title: श्री धनतेरस पूजा स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री धनतेरस पूजा स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ श्री धन्वन्तरि ध्यानम् ॥

नमामि धन्वन्तरिमादिदेवं सुरासुरैर्वन्दितपादपद्मम्।
लोके जरारुग्भयमृत्युनाशं दातारमीशं विविधौषधीनाम्॥ १॥

शंखं चक्रं जलौकां च दधतं अमृतघटम्।
चतुर्भुजं पीतवसं धन्वन्तरिमहं भजे॥ २॥

॥ धनलक्ष्मी स्तुतिः ॥

धनदायिनि धनलक्ष्मि धनधान्यसमृद्धिदे।
धनतेरसि समायाते मम गेहं प्रसीद मे॥ ३॥

हिरण्यवर्णे हरिणि सुवर्णरजतस्रजे।
चन्द्रां हिरण्मयीं लक्ष्मीं जातवेदो म आवह॥ ४॥

कुबेरदत्तधनदां निधीशसंयुतां शुभाम्।
ऐश्वर्यदायिनीं लक्ष्मीं धनतेरसि पूजये॥ ५॥

॥ यमदीपदानम् ॥

मृत्युना पाशदण्डाभ्यां कालेन श्यामलेन च।
त्रयोदश्यां दीपदानात् सूर्यजः प्रीतिमाप्नुयात्॥ ६॥

यमाय धर्मराजाय मृत्यवे चान्तकाय च।
वैवस्वताय कालाय सर्वभूतक्षयाय च॥ ७॥

दीपदानं प्रयच्छामि शमनाय नमोऽस्तु ते।
त्रयोदश्यां प्रदोषे च दीपदानं करोम्यहम्॥ ८॥

॥ धनप्राप्ति प्रार्थना ॥

लक्ष्मीर्देहि धनं देहि सुखं सौभाग्यमेव च।
आरोग्यं देहि मे नित्यं धन्वन्तरे नमोऽस्तु ते॥ ९॥

कनकधाराप्रवाहेन सर्वभद्रं प्रयच्छ मे।
धनतेरसमहापुण्ये लक्ष्मीनारायणौ भजे॥ १०॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥

धनत्रयोदश्यां यस्तु पठेत्स्तोत्रमिदं शुभम्।
धनधान्यसमृद्धिः स्यात् आरोग्यं च लभेत् सदा॥

॥ इति श्री धनतेरस पूजा स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Dhanteras Puja Stotram ||

|| Shri Dhanvantari Dhyanam ||

Namami Dhanvantarim adi-devam surasurair-vandita-pada-padmam |
Loke jara-rug-bhaya-mrityu-nasham dataram isham vividhaushadhinam || 1 ||

Shankham chakram jalaukam cha dadhatam amrita-ghatam |
Chaturbhujam pita-vasam Dhanvantarim aham bhaje || 2 ||

|| Dhana-Lakshmi Stutih ||

Dhanadayini Dhanalakshmi dhana-dhanya-samriddhide |
Dhanaterasi samayate mama geham prasida me || 3 ||

Hiranyavarne harini suvarna-rajata-sraje |
Chandram hiranmayim Lakshmim jatavedo ma avaha || 4 ||

Kubera-datta-dhanadam nidhisha-samyutam shubham |
Aishvarya-dayinim Lakshmim Dhanaterasi pujaye || 5 ||

|| Yama-Dipa-Danam ||

Mrityuna pasha-dandabhyam kalena shyamalena cha |
Trayodashyam dipa-danat suryajah pritim apnuyat || 6 ||

Yamaya Dharmarajaya mrityave chantakaya cha |
Vaivasvataya Kalaya sarva-bhuta-kshayaya cha || 7 ||

Dipa-danam prayachchhami Shamanaya namo'stu te |
Trayodashyam pradoshe cha dipa-danam karomy-aham || 8 ||

|| Dhana-Prapti Prarthana ||

Lakshmir dehi dhanam dehi sukham saubhagyam eva cha |
Arogyam dehi me nityam Dhanvantare namo'stu te || 9 ||

Kanaka-dhara-pravahena sarva-bhadram prayachchha me |
Dhanteras-maha-punye Lakshmi-Narayanau bhaje || 10 ||

|| Phalashrutih ||

Dhana-trayodashyam yastu pathet stotram idam shubham |
Dhana-dhanya-samriddhih syat arogyam cha labhet sada ||

|| Iti Shri Dhanteras Puja Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: धनतेरस के शुभ अवसर पर पठित इस स्तोत्र में तीन प्रमुख भाग हैं। प्रथम भाग में भगवान धन्वन्तरि का ध्यान है जो समुद्र मन्थन से अमृत कलश लेकर प्रकट हुए, सभी रोगों और मृत्यु भय के नाशक हैं। द्वितीय भाग में धनलक्ष्मी की स्तुति है जो धन-धान्य की समृद्धि प्रदान करती हैं। तृतीय भाग में यमराज को दीपदान की विधि है जिससे अकाल मृत्यु का भय दूर होता है। अन्त में धन-आरोग्य-सौभाग्य प्राप्ति की प्रार्थना है। इस स्तोत्र के पाठ से धन-धान्य की वृद्धि और सदा आरोग्य लाभ होता है।
benefits: Invokes blessings of Lord Dhanvantari for health and healing
Attracts wealth and prosperity through Dhana-Lakshmi's grace
Traditionally recited for removal of fear of untimely death through yama-dipa-dana
Invokes gold, silver and material abundance
Invokes the blessing of good health and freedom from diseases
Traditionally associated with auspiciousness on the sacred dhanteras occasion
description: Dhanteras Puja Stotram (श्री धनतेरस पूजा स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to Lord Dhanvantari. This revered text originates from Traditional Puja Literature. Regular recitation is believed to remove fear of untimely death through Yama-dipa-dana, invoke blessings of Lord Dhanvantari for health and healing, and grant gold, silver and material abundance. This prayer is traditionally recited during Dhanteras and Diwali to invoke divine grace. The text contains 10 verses and takes around 6 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Dhanteras Puja Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Dhanteras Puja Stotram in Sanskrit with English transliteration and Hindi meaning. Sacred hymn to Dhanvantari and Lakshmi for wealth and health.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Dhanteras Puja Stotram and what are its three sections?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dhanteras Puja Stotram is a composite ten-verse Sanskrit hymn recited on Dhanteras (Dhana Trayodashi), the first day of the five-day Diwali festival. The stotra is structured in three sections: (1) Dhanvantari Dhyanam — invoking Lord Dhanvantari, the divine physician who emerged from the churning of the ocean carrying the amrita-kalasha; (2) Dhana-Lakshmi Stutih — praising Goddess Mahalakshmi to bestow dhana-dhanya-samriddhi (wealth and grain prosperity); and (3) Yama-Dipa-Danam — the ritual lamp-offering to Yama Dharmaraja to ward off akala-mrityu (untimely death). The hymn closes with a unified prayer for health, wealth, longevity and saubhagya. (Sources: Traditional Puja Literature; Drikpanchang Dhanteras; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of Dhanteras and why is it celebrated?
seoFaqs.answer: Dhanteras (also called Dhana Trayodashi or Dhanvantari Jayanti) falls on the trayodashi (13th tithi) of Krishna Paksha in Kartik month, two days before Diwali. The day commemorates two events: (1) the emergence of Lord Dhanvantari, the physician of the gods and avatar of Vishnu, from the Samudra Manthan carrying the pot of amrita — making this the official day of Ayurveda; and (2) the auspiciousness of Goddess Lakshmi visiting devotees' homes. It marks the start of the five-day Diwali celebration (Dhanteras → Naraka Chaturdashi → Lakshmi Puja → Govardhan Puja → Bhai Dooj). Buying gold, silver, or new utensils on this day is considered to multiply household prosperity. (Sources: Drikpanchang Dhanteras; Hindupedia Dhanvantari Jayanti; Skanda Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What is Yama Deepa Dana and how is it performed?
seoFaqs.answer: Yama Deepa Dana is the unique Dhanteras ritual of offering a deepa (lamp) to Yama Dharmaraja to avert untimely death in the household. As the stotra describes, a chaturmukha (four-wicked) or pancha-batti (five-wicked) earthen lamp is filled with sesame oil, lit with cotton wicks, and placed at the southern doorway of the house (south being Yama's direction) at pradosha-kala (twilight) on Trayodashi. The mantra Mrityuna pasha-dandabhyam kalena shyamalena cha, trayodashyam dipa-danat suryajah pritim apnuyat is recited. This invokes Yama (Suryaja, son of Surya) to be pleased and grant longevity to all family members. The ritual is mentioned in the Padma Purana. (Sources: Padma Purana; Drikpanchang Yama Dipa; Skanda Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Dhanteras puja muhurat in 2026?
seoFaqs.answer: Dhanteras 2026 falls on Saturday, 7 November 2026, with the trayodashi tithi beginning at the previous evening and ending the following day (consult Drikpanchang for the exact regional muhurat). The pradosha-kala muhurat — the most auspicious window for Lakshmi-Kubera-Dhanvantari puja and Yama Deepa Dana — typically lasts about 2 hours after sunset. Devotees observing the full ritual perform abhisheka of Lakshmi-Ganesha-Kubera murtis, light the southern Yama deepa first, then perform the Dhanvantari dhyana with curd-rice naivedya, followed by the Lakshmi sthapana. New gold, silver, brass utensils, brooms or coins purchased earlier in the day are placed before the deities for sanctification. (Sources: Drikpanchang 2026 Dhanteras; Vedic Panchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What should one buy on Dhanteras and why?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition prescribes buying items associated with Lakshmi's grace and Dhanvantari's healing: gold ornaments (most auspicious), silver coins or utensils (Lakshmi's metal), brass and bronze utensils, new brooms (jharu — Lakshmi enters cleaned homes), kitchen items, and Dhanvantari-related Ayurvedic items or copper utensils (the metal of healing). Modern observance also includes electronics, vehicles and property registration. The reason is that Goddess Lakshmi is believed to enter homes on Dhanteras evening — purchases made on this day are sanctified as her blessing-tokens and multiply over the year. The stotra invokes hiranyavarne harini suvarna-rajata-sraje — Lakshmi adorned with gold and silver garlands. (Sources: Drikpanchang Dhanteras shopping; Skanda Purana; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Dhanteras Puja Stotram grant?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti promises that one who recites this auspicious stotra on Dhana Trayodashi obtains dhana-dhanya-samriddhi (wealth and food abundance) and arogya (good health) eternally. Specific benefits invoked across the three sections include: (1) freedom from disease, fear of old age and untimely death (Dhanvantari section); (2) gold-silver abundance, sovereign authority, business expansion, and Mahalakshmi's permanent residence in the home (Lakshmi section); and (3) protection of all family members from akala-mrityu through Yama's grace (Yama-dipa section). The combined recitation is considered the perfect Dhanteras sankalpa. (Sources: Traditional Puja phalashruti; Padma Purana; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Dhanteras Puja Stotram differ from Lakshmi Stotram and Dhanvantari Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: These three hymns serve different ritual purposes around Dhanteras. The Dhanteras Puja Stotram is a composite festival-specific text combining Dhanvantari, Lakshmi, Kubera and Yama invocations — designed for one-day Trayodashi observance. The Lakshmi Stotram (such as the Mahalakshmi Ashtakam, Sri Suktam, Kanakadhara Stotram) is recited throughout the year and especially on Diwali night Lakshmi Puja for sustained wealth-blessings. The Dhanvantari Stotram (the 8-verse Namami Dhanvantarim hymn) is recited on Dhanteras morning by Ayurvedic practitioners and patients seeking healing — it has no Lakshmi or Yama invocations. Devotees often chant the Dhanteras Puja Stotram during the trayodashi puja, then the Lakshmi Stotram on Amavasya, completing the festival cycle. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi Dhanteras corpus; Drikpanchang.)
